items.title: South Korea: Oil shock lifts inflation outlook – Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered economists Chong Hoon Park and Arup Ghosh update their South Korea forecasts following higher Oil prices linked to Middle East tensions. They now see 2026 CPI inflation at 2.4% versus 2.0% previously, with Oil averaging USD 85/bbl.

items.title: China: Growth trimmed, inflation lifted – ABN AMRO
items.summary: ABN AMRO's Senior Economist Arjen van Dijkhuizen reviews China’s macro outlook after the Iran conflict, noting stronger early‑2026 data but slightly lower GDP forecasts.

items.title: Banxico unexpectedly cut rates by 25 basis points to 6.75%
items.summary: Banco de Mexico, known as Banxico, unexpectedly cut the country’s main interest rate from 7% to 6.75% on Thursday on a 3-2 vote, with Deputy Governors Jonathan Heath and Galia Borja voting to keep interest rates unchanged.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar surges as Iran tensions keep markets cautious
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) surged to near 99.90, holding steady as safe-haven demand amid Middle East tensions and rate differentials underpinned the Greenback amid a cautious market mood.

items.title: Asia FX: De‑escalation needed for stability – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lloyd Chan maintains a defensive stance on Asia FX as the US‑Iran conflict keeps external pressures elevated. Higher US yields and Oil prices are supporting the Dollar and weighing on Asian currencies such as THB, PHP and KRW.

items.title: BoE’s Taylor: Currently see a high bar to hiking
items.summary: Alan Taylor, an external member of the Monetary Policy Committee of the Bank of England (BoE), said that he currently sees a high bar to hiking the rates at a conference in New York hosted by Exante Data. He also added that the current energy shock looks more like the 2011 situation than the 2022.

items.title: NZD/USD falls for third straight day as Iran-US tensions lift safe-haven US Dollar
items.summary: NZD/USD extends its downward move on Thursday, trading around 0.5770 at the time of writing, down 0.65% on the day. The pair is now on its third consecutive day of losses after failing to hold near the 0.5900 area reached last week.

items.title: GCC: Shock transmission and buffers – Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered Bank analysts Bader Al Sarraf and Razia Khan assess how the Middle East escalation and potential Strait of Hormuz disruptions could affect Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) economies.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average futures whiplash as Iran tensions resume
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell around 230 points, or roughly 0.5%, on Thursday, giving back the previous session's gains as surging Oil prices and collapsing ceasefire optimism crushed risk appetite. The S&P 500 dropped 0.8%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite shed 1.1%.

items.title: NOK: Norges Bank hawkish shift supports currency – ING
items.summary: ING’s Francesco Pesole notes that Norges Bank delivered a hawkish shift, signalling upcoming rate hikes as inflation concerns extend beyond the energy shock. The bank now fully prices at least one hike in its projections, while ING expects only one move given its bearish Oil and gas baseline.

items.title: Oil: Volatile price action on ceasefire uncertainty – ING
items.summary: ING’s Ewa Manthey and Warren Patterson note that NYMEX WTI and ICE Brent are up around 2% after sharp volatility around $100/bbl as markets react to conflicting US‑Iran ceasefire signals.

items.title: Norges Bank: Higher rate path supports stronger Krone – Nordea
items.summary: Nordea's Chief economist Kjetil Olsen and Senior Macro and FX Strategist Sara Midtgaard note that the central bank of Norway, Norges Bank now signals a policy rate hike to 4.25% at an upcoming meeting, with the new path pointing to 4.25–4.50% by year-end.
